Event (Latin): Iesus Christus in Bethlehem in Iudaeā natus esse dicitur.
Event (English): Jesus Christ is said to have been born in Bethlehem in Judea.

Event (Latin): Augustus Tiberium ad Romam revocat.
Event (English): Augustus recalls Tiberius to Rome.

Event (Latin): Ars Amatoria ab Ovidiō scripta est.
Event (English): Ovid writes the Ars Amatoria.

Event (Latin): Nepos Augusti et heres adoptatus, Gaius Caesar, subito morbo mortuus est.
Event (English): Augustus's grandson and adopted heir Gaius Caesar dies suddenly of illness.

Event (Latin): Rex Herod ā Augusto victus est.
Event (English): King Herod is defeated by Augustus.

Event (Latin): Carmen nomine Metamorphoses ab Ovidiō editus est.
Event (English): Ovid publishes his poem Metamorphoses.

Event (Latin): Varus legātus legionesque a Germanibus in proeliō Teutoburgiense saltūs destructi sunt
Event (English): Varus and his legions are destroyed by the Germans in the battle of the Teutoburg forest.

Event (Latin): Provincia Illyricum in Pannoniam et Dalmatiam divisum est
Event (English): The province of Illyricum is divided into Pannonia and Dalmatia

Event (Latin): Tiberius princeps cum Augustō appellatus est et omnēs suās potestātēs eī dantur.
Event (English): Tiberius is named "princeps" along with Augustus and given all his same powers.

Event (Latin): Augustus mortuus est.
Event (English): Augustus dies.

Event (Latin): Tiberius imperator fit.
Event (English): Tiberius becomes emperor.

Event (Latin): Cappadocia Syriaque a Roma provinciae factae sunt
Event (English): Cappadocia and Syria join the Empire

Event (Latin): Ovidius Naso poeta, qui Metamorphoses scripserat, mortuus est
Event (English): The poet Ovid, who wrote Metamorphoses, died

Event (Latin): Imperator imperium ad Germanicum Iulius Caesarem dedit
Event (English): Germanicus Iulius Caesar is given imperium by the Emperor

Event (Latin): Sucessor Imperatori, nomine Drusus Iulius Caesar, mortuus est
Event (English): Drusus Iulius Caesar, the Emperor's successor, dies

Event (Latin): Tiberius ad Caprem venivit, et imperium Seiano dedit
Event (English): Tiberius retired to Capri, allowing Sejanus to rule in his place

Event (Latin): Livia, mater Tiberii, mortuus est
Event (English): Livia, the mother of Tiberius died

Event (Latin): Tiberius iussit Sejanum interficeretur
Event (English): Tiberius commands Sejanus to be killed

Event (Latin): Tiberius Caligulam ad Caprem invitavit
Event (English): Tiberius invited Caligula to Capri

Event (Latin): Tiberius mortuus est, et Imperium Romanum ad Caligula et Tiberio Gemello dedit
Event (English): Tiberius dies, and gives the Empire to Caligula and Tiberius Gemellus

Event (Latin): Caligula iussit Tiberium Gemellum occideretur
Event (English): Caligula orchestrated the murder of Tiberius Gemellus

Event (Latin): Caligula iussit Regem Mauretaniam occideretur
Event (English): Caligula orders the King of Mauretania to be killed

Event (Latin): Caligula a Cohortibus praetoriae interfectus est
Event (English): Caligula is assassinated by the Praetorian Guard

Event (Latin): Filius Nero Claudius Drusus Imperator fuit
Event (English): The son of Nero Claudius Drusus becomes emperor

Event (Latin): Legiones Romae in Brittaniam transiverunt ut regionem capere incipet
Event (English): Roman Legions arrive in Britain to begin conquering it

Event (Latin): Thracia a Roma provincia facta est
Event (English): Thracia is made a Roman province

Event (Latin): Uxor Imperatori, nomina Messalina, quae nepos Augusti erat, propter coniurationem, occisa est
Event (English): Messalina, wife to the emperor and grand-niece of Augustus, is executed for conspiracy

Event (Latin): Imperator, qui antehac Agrippinam in matrimonio duxerat, filium Neronem suum in familiam Imperatori adoptavit
Event (English): The emperor, who had earlier married Agrippina, adopted her son Nero into the Imperial family

Event (Latin): Nero imperator fuit
Event (English): Nero becomes emperor

Event (Latin): Agrippina, mater Imperatori, a filio suo interfecta est
Event (English): Agrippina, mother to the Emperor, is killed by her son

Event (Latin): Boudicca, regina Iceni, gens in Britanniam, hostes contra Romam duxit
Event (English): Boudicca, queen of the Iceni, a tribe in Britain, leads a rebellion against Rome

Event (Latin): circa octaginta milia personas rebelliones in Brittaniam a militibus Romanibus cecidunt
Event (English): Around 80,000 rebelling people in Britain are killed by Roman soldiers

Event (Latin): sexies diebus incendium Urbem Romam ardivit. Tres inter quattuordecim regiones in quo Roma divisa est deleta sunt, et septem altera regiones incendio afflicti sunt
Event (English): For six days, a fire burned in Rome, which destroyed 3 of the 14 regions of the city, and damaged another 7

Event (Latin): Sanctus Petrus, primus episcopus Romanus a Imperatore cruciatus est
Event (English): Saint Peter, the first bishop of Rome, is crucified by the emperor

Event (Latin): Domus Aurea a Imperatore aedificare inceptus est
Event (English): The Golden House begins being built by the Emperor

Event (Latin): Coniuratio Pisone contra Neronem fortmata est, noniam Nero
Event (English): The Pisonian Conspiracy against Nero is formed

Event (Latin): Nerva coniurationem contra imperatorem exponere adiuvit
Event (English): Nerva helps expose a conspiracy against the emperor

Event (Latin): Imperator iussit Seneca, qui in coniuratio dictus est, se necet
Event (English): The Emperor orders Seneca, who he claimed to be part of a conspiracy, to kill himself

Event (Latin): Gens Iudeae, qui in Hierosolyma vixit, Primum Bellum Iudaicum inceptus est
Event (English): The People of Judea, who inhabited Jerusalem, began the Great Jewish Revolt

Event (Latin): Nero iussit servum suum se occidet
Event (English): Nero commands his slave to kill him

Event (Latin): Galba, rector provinciae Hispaniae Tarraconense, Imperator a Senatu clamatus est
Event (English): Galba, governor of Hispania Terraconensis, is declared Emperor by the Senate

Event (Latin): Annus quattuor imperatorum
Event (English): The Year of the Four Emperors

Event (Latin): Cohortes praetoriae Imperatorem necaverunt ut Othonem imperatorem sit
Event (English): The Praetorian Guard assasinates the Emperor so that Otho could take the throne

Event (Latin): Post Prater Imperatorem vicit, Vitellius se Imperator dixit
Event (English): After defeating the previous emperor, Vitellius announces his claim to the throne

Event (Latin): Vespasianus a Senatu Imperator decretus est
Event (English): Vespasian is declared emperor by the Senate

Event (Latin): Milites qui ad Vespasianum fidelem erat, Vitellem necaverunt
Event (English): Troops loyal to Vespasian killed Vitellius

Event (Latin): Murus ruens Hierosolymae a legionibus Romanis transitus est, et Secundum Templum Hierosolymum destructum est
Event (English): The Walls of Jerusalem fall to Rome's legions, and the Second Temple is destroyed

Event (Latin): Vespasianus vectigalum urinae reinstitit. Pecunia non olet
Event (English): Vespasian renews the urine tax. Money does not stink

Event (Latin): Primum Bellum Idudaicum perfectum est cum Roma Sicarios vicet in obsidio Masadae
Event (English): The Great Jewish result end with the defeat of the Sicarii at the Siege of Masada

Event (Latin): Mons Vesuvius erupit, et Pompeium Herculaneumque destructi sunt
Event (English): Mount Vesuvius erupts, and Pompeii and Herculaneum are destroyed

Event (Latin): Plinius Maior a Vulcano erumpente necatus est
Event (English): Pliny the Elder is killed by a vulcanic eruption

Event (Latin): Titus, filius Imperatori, imperator fuit
Event (English): Titus, son of an emperor, becomes one

Event (Latin): Amphitheatrum Flavio perfectum est
Event (English): The Flavian Amphitheater is completed

Event (Latin): Titus morbo mortuus est
Event (English): Titus dies of fever

Event (Latin): Domitianus imperator fuit
Event (English): Domitian becomes emperor

Event (Latin): Praeter Caledoniam, Gnaeus Iulius Agricola totam Britanniam occupavit
Event (English): Except for Scotland, Gnaeus Iulius Agricola occupied the entirety of Britain

Event (Latin): Domitianus contra Daciam bellum geruit
Event (English): Domitian waged war against Dacia

Event (Latin): Dacia, ubi os fluminis Danuvio et litore Ponto Euxino adiugat, clientis Romani facta est
Event (English): Dacia, where the mouth of the Danube meets the Black Sea is made a client of Rome

Event (Latin): Dynastia Flavio perfecta est
Event (English): The Flavian Dynasty in ended

Event (Latin): Nerva imperator fuit
Event (English): Nerva becomes emperor

Event (Latin): M. Fabius Quintillianus rhetor, qui Institutio Oratoria scripserat, mortuus est
Event (English): The Rhetor Quintillian, who wrote Institutes of Oratory, died

Event (Latin): Traianus a Imperatore adoptus est
Event (English): Trajan is adopted by the emperor

Event (Latin): Traianus imperator fuit
Event (English): Trajan becomes emperor

Event (Latin): Tacitus Agricola scripsit, quem de vita Iulii Agricolae narravit.
Event (English): Tacitus wrote Agricola, which tells of the life of Julius Agricola

Event (Latin): Primum Bellum Daciam inceptum est
Event (English): The First Dacian War began

Event (Latin): Poeta Martialis, qui Epigrammaton scripserat, mortuus est
Event (English): The poet Martial, who had written Epigrams, died

Event (Latin): Plinius Minor in Epistulis amorem magnum uxoris eius, nomina Calpurnia descripsit
Event (English): Pliny the Younger describes the great love of his wife, Calpurnia, in his Epistulae

Event (Latin): Secundum Bellum Daciam inceptum est
Event (English): The Second Dacian War begins

Event (Latin): Arabia Petraea provincia Romana a Traiano facta est
Event (English): Arabia Petraea is made a Roman province by Trajan

Event (Latin): Dacia provincia Romana facta est
Event (English): Dacia is made a Roman province

Event (Latin): Forum Traianum dedicatum est
Event (English): Trajan's forum is completed

Event (Latin): Columna Traiani aedificata est
Event (English): Trajan's column is constructed

Event (Latin): Armenia provincia Romana facta est
Event (English): Armenia is made a Roman province

Event (Latin): Cum Mesopotamia Assyriaque provinciae Romanae fecerunt, Imperium Romanum maximum magnitudem fuit
Event (English): When Mesopotamia and Assyria were made Roman provinces, The Roman Empire reached its largest size

Event (Latin): Traianus mortuus est
Event (English): Trajan dies

Event (Latin): Hadrianur imperator fuit
Event (English): Hadrian becomes emperor

Event (Latin): Suetonius De vita Caesarum scripsit, quem de vita imperatoris ab Iulio Caesare ad Domitianum narravit
Event (English): Suetonius wrote The Twelve Caesars, which biographs the emperors from Julius Caesar to Domitian

Event (Latin): Vallum Hadriani aedificare inceptum est
Event (English): Hadrian's Wall begins construction

Event (Latin): rebellio in Mauretaria contra Imperium Romanum a Hadriano victa est
Event (English): A rebellion in Mauretania against Roman rule is put down by Hadrian

Event (Latin): Hadrianus, in Graecia, invitavit nobiles e Athenis Spartaque in Senatu
Event (English): Hadrian, while in Greece, invited Nobles from Athens and Sparta to the Roman Senate

Event (Latin): Post mortem Antinoum, amatorem Imperatori, Imperator Panhellenionem fecit
Event (English): After the death of his lover, Antinous, the emperor founded the Panhellenion

Event (Latin): Simon bar Kokhba rebellionem contra Imperium Romae in Judeae duxit
Event (English): Simon bar Kokhba leads a rebellion against Roman rule in Judea

Event (Latin): Lucius Aelius, qui a Hadriano adoptus erat, mortuus est. Tunc Hadrianus Antoninum Pium adoptavit cum is Marcum Aurelium adoptare promitteret
Event (English): Lucius Aelius, who had been adopted by Hadrian, passed away. Then, Hadrian adopted Antoninus Pius when he promised to adopt Marcus Aurelius

Event (Latin): Post mortem Faustinae, ea deam a Senatu facta est, et maritum eum templum in foro aedificare inceptum est
Event (English): After the death of Faustina, she was deified, and her husband began building a temple to her in the forum

Event (Latin): Imperator qualitatem argentum denarii decrevit ut festivalem nonagesimo anno Urbis Romae celebri potesset
Event (English): The Emperor debased the quality of Silver denarii in order to celebrate the 900th year of the city of Rome

Event (Latin): Vallum Antoninum, sexuaginta et septem milia septentrionis vallum Hadriani, aedificatum est
Event (English): The Antonine Wall, 67 miles north of Hadrian's wall, is builty

Event (Latin): In mortuo, Antoninus Pius Imperium Romanum a Filiis Marco Aurelio et Lucio Vero dedit
Event (English): In death, Antoninus Pius gave the empire to his sons Marcus Aurelius and Lucius Verus

Event (Latin): Pestis Antonina, quae infinitum numerum hominem necatura fuit, incepit
Event (English): The Antonine Plague, which would kill a large amount of the Roman population, begins

Event (Latin): Prima littera de Grege Romani in cohorte Imperatori Sinae esse scripta est
Event (English): The first record of a group of Romans being in the court of the Emperor of China is written

Event (Latin): Lucius Verus morbo mortuus est
Event (English): Lucius Verus dies of disease

Event (Latin): Bellum Germanicum et Sarmaticum, quid reliquum regnum Marci Aurelii constavit, incepit
Event (English): The Marcomannic Wars, which would last the rest of Marcus Aurelius's reign, begins

Event (Latin): Miraculus Imbris Legiones Romani in Regione Marcomanni servavit
Event (English): The miracle of the rain saves roman legions in Marcomannia

Event (Latin): Statua Equestra Aenea Marci Aurelii, quae usque ad nos pervenit, creata est
Event (English): The Equestrian statue of Marcus Aurelius, one of the only imperial equestrian statues that survives to this day, is made

Event (Latin): Commodus imperator cum patre factus est
Event (English): Commodus is made emperor with his father

Event (Latin): Marco Aurelio mortuo, Pax Romana perfecta est
Event (English): With Marcus Aurelius' death, the Pax Romana ends

Event (Latin): Commodus solus Imperator fuit
Event (English): Commodus becomes sole Emperor

Event (Latin): Soror Commodi, nomina Lucillia, coniurationem contra Imperatorem fecit
Event (English): Lucilla, the sister of Commodus, built a conspiracy against the emperor

Event (Latin): Legiones Romae a Vallo Antonino reliquerunt
Event (English): Roman Legions abandon the Antonine Wall

Event (Latin): Pescennius Niger, qui imperator futurus fuit, contra Daciam praestantiter pugnavit
Event (English): Pescennius Niger, who would become Emperor, is commended for his fighting against the Dacians

Event (Latin): Cleander, consuasor Commodi, magistratus vendere pro ipse incepit
Event (English): Cleander, advisor of Commodus, begins selling off public offices for his own benefit

Event (Latin): Commodus nomines Mensium de nominibus suis mutavit. Nunc: Lucius, Aelius, Aurelius, Commodus, Augustus, Herculeus, Romanus, Exsuperatorius, Amazonius, Invictus, Felix, et Pius
Event (English): Commodus renames the months after himself. Now they are: Lucius, Aelius, Aurelius, Commodus, Augustus, Herculeus, Romanus, Exsuperatorius, Amazonius, Invictus, Felix, and Pius

Event (Latin): Commodus Mortuus a Senatu de facto damnatio memoriae decretus est
Event (English): Commodus is declared a public enemy by the senate, following his death

Event (Latin): Pertinax a Cohortibus Praetoriae imperator dictus est. Hic eventus Annum quinque imperatores incepit.
Event (English): Pertinax is delcared Emperor by the Praetorian Guard. This event begins the year of the Five Emperors

Event (Latin): Pertinace Mortuo, Cohotres Praetoriae Imperatorem Didio Juliano vendiverunt
Event (English): With Pertinax dead, the Praetorian Guard sells the Emperorship to Didius Julianus

Event (Latin): Pescennius Niger et Septimius Severus a Legiones Imperatorem decreti sunt
Event (English): Pescennius Niger and Septimius Severus are declared Emperor by their legions

Event (Latin): In hoc anno, Pertinax, Didius Julianus, Pescennius Niger, Clodius Albinus, et Septimius Severus Imperatores fuerunt
Event (English): In this year, Pertinax, Didius Julianus, Pescennius Niger, Clodius Albinus, and Septimius Severus become Emperor

Event (Latin): Pescennius Niger, qui imperatorm se dixit, a Imperatore Septimio Severo interfectus est
Event (English): Pescennius Niger, who declared himself emperor, is killed by the emperor Septimius Severus

Event (Latin): Clodius Albinus, qui imperatorm se dixit, a Imperatore Septimio Severo interfectus est
Event (English): Clodius Albinus, who declared himself emperor, is killed by the emperor Septimius Severus

Event (Latin): Filius Imperatori, nomine Caracalla, a Imperatore Imperatorem decretus est
Event (English): The son of the emperor, who was named Caracalla, is declared emperor by the emperor

Event (Latin): Caracalla a Imperatore iussus est Fulviam Plautillam, quae ad ipso odi est, in matrimonio ducat
Event (English): Caracalla is commanded by the emperor to marry Fulvia Plautilla, whom he hated.

Event (Latin): Sancta Perpetua Carthaginis mortua est
Event (English): Saint Perpetua died in Carthage

Event (Latin): Septimio Severo Mortuo, Filii Caracalla Getaque imperatores fuit. Posthac, Decembri, Cohortes Praetoriae Getam interfecerunt
Event (English): With Septimius Severus dead, his sons, Caracalla and Geta, are made emperors. After this, in December, the Praetorian Guard kill Geta

Event (Latin): Caracalla Imperator Constitutionem Antoninianam iussit. Haec lex libertos cives Romanos fecit, et libertabus civitates feminae Romanae dedit
Event (English): Emperor Caracalla passes the Constituio Antoniniana. This law makes freedmen Roman citizens, and gives freedwomen the same rights as Roman women

Event (Latin): Dum Caracalla regnavit, ipse iussit nummos Imperio Romano monetet cum simulacris dei Aegypti Serapis
Event (English): While Caracalla ruled, he commanded that Roman coins be printed with the likeness of the Egyptian God Serapis

Event (Latin): Caracalla a Cohortibus Praetoriae occisus est
Event (English): Caracalla is killed by the Praetorian Guard

Event (Latin): Elegabalus Imperatorem Macrinum interfecit
Event (English): Elegabalus kills Emperor Macrinus

Event (Latin): Sacerdos Elagabali, deus solis Syriae, ad Romam ivit, et Imperator fuit
Event (English): A priest of Elagabal, a Syrian sun god, went to Rome and became Emperor

Event (Latin): Iuppiter de Ducem Panthei Romanae mutavit pro Elagabalum
Event (English): Jupiter is changed from head of the Roman Pantheon to Elagabal

Event (Latin): Imperator Virginem Vestalem Aquilam Severam in matrimonio duxit
Event (English): The emperor marries a vestal virgin, named Aquilia Severa

Event (Latin): Elegabalus, cum Matre sui, a Cohortibus Praetoriae occisus est, et frater imperatori, nomine Severus Alexander, imperator fuit
Event (English): Elegabalus and his mother are killed by the Praetorian Guard, and his cousin, Severus Alexander, is declared Emperor

Event (Latin): Ante Imperatore Marco Antonii Giordiani, Severus Alexander Imperator Iuvenissimus fuit
Event (English): Before Emperor Marcus Antonius Giordianus, Severus Alexander becomes the youngest emperor

Event (Latin): Cohortes praetoriae praefectum Ulpianum interfecit
Event (English): The Praetorian Guard killed their prefect, Ulpian

Event (Latin): Legio XXII Primigenia consternavit et Imperatorem Severum Alexandrem interfect
Event (English): The 22nd Legion Primigenia mutinied and killed the emperor Severus Alexander

Event (Latin): Severo Alexandro Necato, Discrimen tertii saeculi inceptum est
Event (English): With Severus Alexander killed, the Crisis of the Third Century begins

Event (Latin): Maximinus Thrax, primus Imperator qui nec ex senatoribus nec ex equitibus esse, Imperator fuit
Event (English): Maximinus Thrax, the first emperor to not be from the Senatorial or Equestrian class, becomes emperor

Event (Latin): Post rebellionem in Africa, Senatus Gordianum filium suumque Imperatores appelavit. Cum imperator Maximinus Thrax hunc eventum audiret, legiones ad Romam duxit
Event (English): After a rebellion in Africa, the Senate declared Gordian and his son emperors. When Emperor Maximinus Thrax heard this, he led his legions to Rome

Event (Latin): Capelianus, Legatus Augusti pro praetore in Numidia, Imperatores Gordianum Gordianum Secundumque occisit. Cum senatus cognovisset, Imperium ad Pupienum Balbinumque dedit
Event (English): Capelianus, Legate Augusti Pro Pratore in Numidia, killed the emperors Gordian I and Gordian II. When the Senate learns of this, they declare Pupienus and Balbinus emperors

Event (Latin): Cum populus Urbis Romae contra Pupienum Balbinumque rebelavisset, Imperatori Gordianum Tertium imperium dederunt ut turbas placent
Event (English): When the populace of the City of Rome rebelled against Pupienus and Balbinus, the Emperors declared Gordian III Emperor to calm the crowds

Event (Latin): Maximinus Thrax a militibus sui ocissus est. Postea, Pupienus et Balbinus a Cohortibus praetoriae occisi sunt. Tunc, Gordianus Tertius solus imperator fuit
Event (English): Maximinus Thrax is killed by his troops. Afterwards, Pupienus and Balbinus are killed by the Praetorian Guard, leaving Gordian III as sole Emperor

Event (Latin): Gordianus Tertius, anno undevingesimo, bellum contra Regnum Sassanidarum vicit, et triumphalem celebravit
Event (English): A 18 year old Gordian III celebrated a triumph for his victory over the Sassanid Empire

Event (Latin): Postquam Triumphalem, Gordianus Tertius in proelium mortuus est. Marcus Iulius Philippus, praefectus Cohortium Praetoriae, imperator fuit
Event (English): After his triumph, Gordian III dies in battle. Philip the Arab, prefect of the Praetorian Guard, became emperor

Event (Latin): Roma annum milium ab urbe condita celebravit
Event (English): Rome celebrated its thousandth year of existence

Event (Latin): Gaius Messius Quintus Decius Imperatorem Marcum Iulius Philippum Arabem in proelio vicit
Event (English): Decius defeats Emperor Philip the Arab in battle

Event (Latin): Proelium apud Arbitrum, Imperator Decium a Gothos ocissus est. Trebonianus Gallus a legionibus sui Imperium datus est
Event (English): At the Battle of Arbitrus, Emperor Decius is killed by the Goths. Rebonianus Gallus is declared emperor by his legions

Event (Latin): Imperator Gallus et filius suus, nomine Volusianus, in consternatio occisi sunt. Aemelianus a Legionibus suis Imperidum datus est
Event (English): The Emperor Gallus and his son, Volusianus, are killed in a mutiny, and Aemelianus is declared emperor by his troops

Event (Latin): Postquam occisum Imperatori Aemeliani, Valerianus Imperium a Legione suis datus est
Event (English): After the murder of the Emperor Aemelianus, Valerian is declared emperor by his legion

Event (Latin): Postquam Regnum Sassanidarum Antiochum occupavit, Imperator Valerian urbem liberavit
Event (English): After the Sassanid Empire occupied Antioch, the Emperor Valerian liberated the city

Event (Latin): Exercitus Regna Sassanidarum in Proelio apud Edessam Imperatorem Valerianum vicit et cepit
Event (English): At the battle of Edessa, the Sassanid army defeated the Emperor Vespasian and made him a prisoner of war

Event (Latin): Postumus a legionibus suis imperium datus est. Sed non imperator Imperio Romano fuit. Imperium Gallicum creavit
Event (English): Postumus is declared emperor by his legions, but he does not rule in the Roman empire. He creates the Gallic Empire

Event (Latin): Valerianus, captivus Regni Sassanidarum, in captivo mortuus est. Tunc, filius Valerianus, nomine Gallienus, solus imperator fuit
Event (English): Valerian, prisoner of the Sassanid Empire, dies in captivity. Then, his son, Gallienus, became the sole Emperor

Event (Latin): Marito Zenobiae Mortuo, Septimia Zenobia, mater regi et regina Palmyrene, Imperium Palmyrenem creavit in Syria
Event (English): With Zenobia's husband dead, Septimia Zenobia, mother of the king and regent of Palmyra, created the Palmyrene Empire in Syria

Event (Latin): Legiones Claudium Gothicum imperium dederunt
Event (English): Legions declared Claudius Gothicus emperor

Event (Latin): Postquam Postumus Mortuus est, Marcus Piavonius Victorinus imperator Gallicorum fuit
Event (English): After Postumus died, Marcus Piavonius Victorinus became Gallic Emperor

Event (Latin): Postquam Claudius Gothicus et frater Quintillius mortui sunt, Lucius Domitius Aurelianus imperator fuit
Event (English): After the deaths of Claudius Gothicus and his brother Quintillius, Aurelian is declared emperor

Event (Latin): Tetricus Primus imperator Gallicorum fuit
Event (English): Tetricus I became Gallic Emperor

Event (Latin): Aurelianus Imperium Palmyrenem destruxit
Event (English): Aurelian destroys the Palmyrene Empire

Event (Latin): Aurelianus in proelio apud Catalaunicam Imperium Gallicorum vicit et Imperium Romanum reunivit
Event (English): Aurelian defeated the Gallic Empire at the battle of Châlons and reunited the Roman Empire

Event (Latin): Postquam Aurelianus occisus est, Tacitus imperator fuit
Event (English): After Aurelian is murdered, Tacitus became emperor

Event (Latin): Marcus Annius Florianus imperator fuit
Event (English): Florianus became Emperor

Event (Latin): Marcus Aurelius Probus, frater Imperatori Taciti, anno sexigesimo imperiis suis, occisus est
Event (English): Marcus Aurelius Probus, the brother of Emperor Tacitus, in his sixth year of Emperorship, is killed

Event (Latin): Diocletianus a legionibus nomen augustus datus est
Event (English): Diocletian is given the title Augustus by his legions

Event (Latin): Diocletianus Maximianum, legatum imperatori, Caesarem fecit
Event (English): Diocletian makes Maximian, his legate, Caesar

Event (Latin): Bahram Secundus, Rex Regni Sassanidarum, Diocletianum donum dedit, et amicitiam inter imperios clamavit
Event (English): Bahram II, King of the Sassanian Empire, gave Diocletian gifts and declared friendship between the Empires

Event (Latin): Imperator Diocletianus iussit Flavius Constantius exercitum contra Carausium defecentem ducat
Event (English): The Emperor Diocletian commands that Flavius Constantinus lead an army against the rebelling Carausium

Event (Latin): Diocletianus tetrarchiam fecit. Imperium Romanum inter quattuor viri, duos augustos, duos caesares. Primi Augusti sunt Diocletianus et Maximinus. Primi Caesari sunt Galerius et Constantinus
Event (English): Diocletian creates the Tetrarchy. The Roman Empire is divided between four men, two Augusti and two Caesars. The first Augusti are Diocletian and Maximian. The first Caesars are Galerius and Constantius

Event (Latin): Res publica Sancti Marini conditus est
Event (English): San Marino is founded

Event (Latin): Postquam haruspicina non potesse lecta est, Diocletianus et Galerius iussunt persecutio Christiani incepta sit
Event (English): After a haruspicy cannot be read, Diocletian and Galerius begin a large persecution of Christians

Event (Latin): Propter morbo Diocletiani, Augustus imperium abcessit. Flavius Valerius Severus et Maximinus caesares fuit, et Constantius et Galerius augusti fuit
Event (English): Because of Diocletian's illness, he abdicated his throne. Flavius Valerius Severus and Maximinus became Caesars, and Constantius and Galerius became Augusti

Event (Latin): Postquam Constantius mortuus est, filius suus, nomine Constantinus, qui Constantinus Magnus futurus fuit, caesar fuit
Event (English): After the death of Constantius, his son, Costantine, who would later become Constantine the Great, became Caesar

Event (Latin): Bella Civiles Tetrarchiae incepit
Event (English): The Civil Wars of the Tetrarchy begin

Event (Latin): Maxentius, qui a civilibus urbis Romae princeps invictus appelatus est, Severum, Galerium, et Maximianum vicit
Event (English): Maxentius, who was named undefeated prince by the people of the city of Rome, defeated Severus, Galerius, and Maximian

Event (Latin): Postquam Constantinum vincere conatus est, Maximianus, augustus Diocletiani, se occidit
Event (English): After an attempted coup of Constantine, Maximian, Diocletian's Augustus, commits suicide

Event (Latin): Galerius persecutionem Christiani desavit quia Christiani religionem non mutaverunt. Edicto Anuntio, Galerius brevi mortuus est
Event (English): Galerius stops persecution of Christians because the Christians did not change their religion. Shortly after this edict, Galerius passed away

Event (Latin): Cum Maxentio Libri Sibyllini inimicum Romanorum moriturus esse dixissent, is exercitum contra Constantinum duxit, sed victus est
Event (English): When the Sibyllene books told Maxentius that the enemy of the Romans would perish, he led his army against Constantine, but was defeated

Event (Latin): In somno, Constantinus in scutis legiis Chi-Rho pingere iustus est. In Proelio apud pontem Milvii, Exercitus Magnus Maxentii a exercitu parvis Constantini victus est
Event (English): In a dream, Constantine is told to paint the Chi-Rho on his soldiers' shields. In the battle of the Milvian Bridge, the outnumbered Constantine forces defeated Maxentius's

Event (Latin): Edictum Mediolanense a Constantino Magno conventum est, quid dixit Christianos et alteros homines religionium deum suum precari potesse, et homines omnium religionium propitia esse
Event (English): The Edict of Milan is passed by Constantine the Great, which allows Christians and any members of religions to practice them, and that they will be held in high regard

Event (Latin): Postquam Licinius Maximinum Dazam vicerat, Constantinus Magnus Licinium in proelio apud Cibalas vicit
Event (English): After Licinius had defeated Maximinus Daza, Constantine defeats Licinius at the Battle of Cibalae

Event (Latin): in primo preolio apud Hadrianopolem, legiones Constantini Magni multos milites Licinii occiderunt. Illo anno, exercitus Licinii tandem totaliter a Constantino Magno victus est
Event (English): At the first Battle of Adrianople, Constantine the Great's legions kill a large amount of Licinius's soldiers. That year, Licinius's army is finally completely defeated by Constantine's army

Event (Latin): In Consilio Nicaeno Primo, Natura Iesu Christu expositus est, quae Iesum filium Dei dixit
Event (English): At the first council of Nicaea, The relationship of Jesus and God the Father is established, which expresses Jesus as the son of God

Event (Latin): Postquam Bella civiles Tetrarchiae prefectum est, Constantinus Magnus solus imperator fuit
Event (English): After the end of the Civil Wars of the Tetrarchy, Constantine the Great became sole Emperor

Event (Latin): Urbs Amplissima Imperii Romani a Constantino Magno in Byzantio translata est. Constantinus Magnus hanc urbem Constantinopolem renominavit
Event (English): The Capital of the Roman Empire is moved to Byzantium by Constantine the Great. He renames this city Constantinople

Event (Latin): Constantino Magno Mortuo, Imperium Romanum in tres partibus divisum est, inter filios Constantini Magni, qui nomines Constantinus Secundus, Constantius Secundus, et Constans sunt
Event (English): With Constantine the Great dead, the Roman Empire was spit between his three sons, whose names were Constantine II, Constantius II, and Constans

Event (Latin): Constantinus Secundus contra fratrem suum exercitum duxerat, sed in proelio sequendo mortuus est
Event (English): Constantine II had led an army against his brother, but died in the subsequent battle

Event (Latin): Legionibus Iratis, Magnentius Imperium datus est, et Legiones contra Constantem duxit
Event (English): With the Legions angry, Magnentius is declared emperor, and he led Legions against Constans

Event (Latin): In preolio apud Montem Seleucum, Constanius Secundus Magnentium vicit
Event (English): At the Battle of Mons Seleucus, Constantius II defeated Magnentius

Event (Latin): Constantio Secundo morbo mortuo, Iulianus solus imperator fuit
Event (English): With Constantius II dead of fever, Julian became the sole Emperor

Event (Latin): Valentinianus Magnus imperator fuit
Event (English): Valentinian the Great became emperor

Event (Latin): Barbaratica Coniuratio, contra Valentinianum Magnum in Britanniam inceptus est. Duces Romani a Barbaris necati sunt
Event (English): The Great Conspiracy against Valentinian the Great in Britain is begun. Roman leaders are killed by local tribes

Event (Latin): In secundo proelio apud Hadrianopolem, exercitus Gothici Alanique urbem petiverunt
Event (English): At the second Battle of Adrianople, Gothic and Alanic armies attacked the city

Event (Latin): Theodosius Magnus Edictum Thessalonicense edidit. Hoc edictum cultum Christianismi religionem Imperii Romani fecit
Event (English): Theodosius the Great Wrote the Edict of Thessalonica. This Edict made Christianity the Empire's religion

Event (Latin): Simulacrum Traiani super columna ea a Cristianis remotum est
Event (English): The statue of Trajan atop his column is removed by Christians

Event (Latin): Ultimi Ludi Olympici Antiqui celebrati sunt
Event (English): The last Ancient Olympic Games are held

Event (Latin): Postquam Theodosius Magnus mortuus est, Imperium Romanum in duas partibus divisum est. Post hunc eventum, Imperium Romanum numquam reunitum est
Event (English): After the death of Theodosius the Great, the Roman Empire is split into two parts. After this event, the Empire would never be reunited

Event (Latin): Aelia Eudoxia a Imperatore Augusta facta est
Event (English): Aelia Eudoxia is made Augusta by the Emperor

Event (Latin): Ultima Munera Gladiatoria in Colosseo edata sunt
Event (English): The Last Gladiator fights are held in the Colosseum

Event (Latin): Sanctus Hieronymus Biblia Vulgata scripsit
Event (English): Saint Jerome writes the Vulgate

Event (Latin): Multa gentes barbarii sine mora trans flumen rhenum in Galliam invaserunt
Event (English): Many Germanic tribes all at once crossed the Rhine river to invade Gaul

Event (Latin): Alaricus, primus rex Visigothorum, exercitum ad Romam duxit ut urbem expugnaret
Event (English): Alaric, the first king of the Visigoths, led an army to Rome and sacked the city

Event (Latin): Ultimi Milites Romani ab Britannia exiverunt. Numquam revenient
Event (English): The last Roman troops leave Britain. They will never return

Event (Latin): Urbs Venetia condita est
Event (English): The city of Venice is founded

Event (Latin): Valentinianus Tertius imperator fuit. Regnum suum anno trecesimo extendet
Event (English): Valentinian III became emperor. He will reign for thirty years despite civil war and a collapsing empire

Event (Latin): In preolio apud Utum, Attila, rex Hunnorum, Daciam invasit
Event (English): At the Battle of Utus, Attila the Hun invades Dacia

Event (Latin): Postquam Pontifex Maximus Leo Attilam dissuasit, Attila decrevit ne Italiam invaderet
Event (English): After Pope Leo dissuaded Atilla, Atilla decided against invading Italy

Event (Latin): Vandales Romam expugnaverunt
Event (English): The Vandals sack Rome

Event (Latin): Eudoxia et Gallia Placibia imperatrices, a Vandalibus captae sunt
Event (English): Empresses Eudoxia and Gallia Placibia are kidnapped by the Vandals

Event (Latin): Ricimer, magister militum, Imperium in nomine Imperatoris Libius Sevērī.
Event (English): Ricimer, Magister Militum of the Roman Empire, led the Empire. Shortly after his death, the Western Roman Empire fell

Event (Latin): Magister Militum Imperiī Occidentalis, Ricimer, exercitum contrā Imperatōrem Anthemium ducit et Olybrium in locō suō imperātorem facit.
Event (English): The Magister Militum of the Western Empire, Ricimer, leads the army against the emperor Anthemius and makes Olybrius emperor in his place.

Event (Latin): Orestes, magister militum, filio suo Imperium dedit. Filius ultimus imperator Imperii Romani Occidentale futurus est
Event (English): Orestes, the magister militum, Declares his son Emperor. This son will become the last Emperor of the Western Roman Empire

Event (Latin): Flavius Odoacer Imperatorem Imperii Romani Occidentale vicit, et Rex Italliam fuit
Event (English): Odoacer defeated the Western Roman Emperor, and became king of Italy

Event (Latin): Imperium Romanum Occidentale cecidit
Event (English): The Western Roman empire fell
